meta.title: Begrüßung und Vorstellung
meta.description: Basic greetings and self-introduction in German: saying hello, stating your name, and simple courteous exchanges.
meta.description_ko: 독일어 기본 인사와 자기소개: 인사하기, 이름 말하기, 간단한 예의 표현.

explanation.en: "Guten Tag" is the standard greeting used during the day in German.
explanation.ko: 「Guten Tag」는 낮 시간에 쓰는 표준 인사말이다.

questions.prompt: Was bedeutet „Guten Tag“? (What does "Guten Tag" mean?)

explanation.en: "Danke" means "thank you".
explanation.ko: 「Danke」는 '감사합니다'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: Was bedeutet „Danke“? (What does "Danke" mean?)

explanation.en: "Ich heiße" means "My name is", used to introduce oneself.
explanation.ko: 「Ich heiße」는 자기소개할 때 쓰는 '제 이름은 ~입니다'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: Was bedeutet „Ich heiße“? (What does "Ich heiße" mean?)

explanation.en: "Auf Wiedersehen" means "goodbye", said when parting.
explanation.ko: 「Auf Wiedersehen」는 헤어질 때 쓰는 '안녕히 가세요'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: Wann sagt man „Auf Wiedersehen“? (When do you say "Auf Wiedersehen"?)

explanation.en: "Wie geht es Ihnen?" means "How are you?" (formal).
explanation.ko: 「Wie geht es Ihnen?」는 (격식체) '어떻게 지내세요?'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: Was bedeutet „Wie geht es Ihnen?“? (What does "Wie geht es Ihnen?" mean?)

explanation.en: "Ich heiße" (the verb heißen) is used to state one's name.
explanation.ko: 「Ich heiße」는 heißen 동사를 사용해 이름을 말하는 표현이다.

questions.prompt: Guten Tag, ich ___ Maria. (Hello, my name is Maria.)

explanation.en: "Wie geht es Ihnen?" is the formal way to ask "How are you?"
explanation.ko: 「Wie geht es Ihnen?」는 격식체로 '어떻게 지내세요?'라고 묻는 표현이다.

questions.prompt: Wie geht es ___? (How are you? — formal)

explanation.en: "Gut" means "well", a common response to how one is doing.
explanation.ko: 「Gut」는 '잘'이라는 뜻으로 안부 인사에 대한 흔한 답변이다.

questions.prompt: Mir geht es ___, danke. (I'm doing well, thank you.)

explanation.en: "Kennenzulernen" means "to get to know", part of the formal greeting phrase.
explanation.ko: 「Kennenzulernen」는 '알게 되다'라는 뜻으로 격식체 인사 표현의 일부이다.

questions.prompt: Freut mich, Sie kennenzu___. (Pleased to meet you.)

explanation.en: "Und du" means "and you", used to redirect the question back to the other person.
explanation.ko: 「Und du」는 '그리고 너는'이라는 뜻으로 상대방에게 질문을 되돌릴 때 쓰인다.

questions.prompt: Und ___, wie geht es dir? (And you, how are are you?)

explanation.en: "Aus" means "from", used to state one's origin.
explanation.ko: 「Aus」는 '~에서, ~출신'이라는 뜻으로 출신을 말할 때 쓰인다.

questions.prompt: Ich komme ___ Korea. (I am from Korea.)

explanation.en: "Name" means "name"; "Wie ist Ihr Name?" asks someone's name formally.
explanation.ko: 「Name」는 '이름'을 뜻하며 '당신의 이름은 무엇입니까?'라는 격식체 질문이다.

questions.prompt: Wie ist Ihr ___? (What is your name? — formal)

explanation.en: "Bis bald" means "see you soon", a casual farewell.
explanation.ko: 「Bis bald」는 '곧 만나요'라는 뜻의 캐주얼한 작별 인사이다.

questions.prompt: Bis ___! (See you later!)

explanation.en: "Ich freue mich" (I am happy) is a reflexive verb expressing pleasure.
explanation.ko: 「Ich freue mich」는 재귀동사로 기쁨을 나타낸다.

questions.prompt: Ich ___ mich, Sie kennenzulernen. (I am happy to make your acquaintance.)

explanation.en: "Entschuldigen Sie bitte" is the formal way to say "excuse me".
explanation.ko: 「Entschuldigen Sie bitte」는 격식체 '실례합니다'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: Entschuldigen Sie ___, wo ist die Toilette? (Excuse me, where is the restroom?)

explanation.en: "War" is the past tense of "sein", used to describe a past state.
explanation.ko: 「War」는 'sein'의 과거형으로 과거 상태를 나타낸다.

questions.prompt: Gern geschehen, es ___ mir eine Freude. (You're welcome, it was a pleasure.)

explanation.en: "Wie geht es dir?" is the informal way to ask "How are you?"
explanation.ko: 「Wie geht es dir?」는 비격식체로 '어떻게 지내?'라고 묻는 표현이다.

questions.prompt: Guten Abend, wie ___ es dir heute Abend? (Good evening, how are you doing tonight? — informal)

explanation.en: "Wie geht's?" is a very common casual way to ask how someone is doing.
explanation.ko: 「Wie geht's?」는 매우 흔한 캐주얼 안부 인사이다.

questions.prompt: Hallo! Wie ___'s? (Hi! How's it going? — casual)

explanation.en: "Aus" means "from", used with countries of origin.
explanation.ko: 「Aus」는 출신 국가와 함께 '~에서'라는 뜻으로 쓰인다.

questions.prompt: Ich bin ___ Korea. (I am from Korea.)

explanation.en: "Kennenlernen" (to get to know) is a separable verb; "kennen zu lernen" appears in the infinitive construction.
explanation.ko: 「Kennenlernen」(알게 되다)는 분리동사이며 'kennen zu lernen' 형태로 부정사 구문에 쓰인다.

questions.prompt: Schön, dich ___ zu lernen! (Nice to get to know you! — informal)

explanation.en: "Heißen" (to be named) does not need "ist" added; the correct form is simply "Ich heiße Peter".
explanation.ko: 「Heißen」동사는 「ist」를 추가할 필요가 없으며 올바른 형태는 'Ich heiße Peter'이다.

questions.prompt: Ich heiße ist Peter. (My name is Peter.) — find the error

explanation.en: In this question, "es" must come before "Ihnen": "geht es Ihnen" (not "geht Ihnen es").
explanation.ko: 이 의문문에서는 'es'가 'Ihnen' 앞에 와야 하므로 'geht es Ihnen'이 맞다.

questions.prompt: Wie geht Ihnen es? (How are you? — formal) — find the error

explanation.en: "Aus" (from) is the correct preposition for stating one's country of origin, not "von".
explanation.ko: 출신 국가를 말할 때는 'von'이 아니라 'aus'를 써야 한다.

questions.prompt: Ich komme von Korea. (I am from Korea.) — find the error

explanation.en: The sentence reads: Ich heiße Maria ("My name is Maria").
explanation.ko: 문장은 'Ich heiße Maria' (제 이름은 마리아입니다)가 된다.

questions.prompt: __________ Maria. (My name is Maria.)

explanation.en: The sentence reads: Mir geht es gut, danke ("I'm doing well, thank you").
explanation.ko: 문장은 'Mir geht es gut, danke' (잘 지내요, 감사합니다)가 된다.

questions.prompt: __________, danke. (I'm doing well, thank you.)
